steps: Go to https://app.cronofy.com/sign_up/developer and create a free developer account
After signing in, navigate to the Developer Dashboard
Click 'Create Application' at the bottom of the application list page
Enter a name and description for your application and choose the data center (US or EU)
Save the application to generate your Client ID and Client Secret
Copy both the Client ID and Client Secret from the application Settings page
Use these credentials in the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow to obtain access tokens for calendar users

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Access Token
credential_fields.description: A Cronofy OAuth access token obtained via the OAuth2 flow (Bearer token). See notes for how to obtain one.

notes: A free developer account includes sandbox credentials for testing all calendar APIs
The Cronofy API uses OAuth 2.0 — users must authorize your app to access their calendars
If client_secret is compromised in production, contact support@cronofy.com immediately
Cronofy supports Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple iCloud, and Exchange
Choose EU or US data center based on your users' location for compliance
